titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in GA 070
GA 070
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

altIdentifier.idno: United Kingdom, London, British Library, Add. 34274, 1 fol.
United Kingdom, Oxford, Bodleian Library, Clarendon Press b.2, fols. 12-19, 26

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2000 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. Further information about the transcription procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition of the majuscules at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Ab tag moved to after witness end tag at 17.1 by Rachel Kevern.
Lac rest of verse added at 17.1 Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 5.39 by Rachel Kevern.
[2]ετε changed to [εχ]ετε at 16.33 by Rachel Kevern.
'rest of verse' tag added after block marker of 16.33 by Rachel Kevern.
[εξ]ετε changed to [2]ετε at 16.33 by Rachel Kevern.
Word breaks corrected in 8.43, 46, by David Parker.
Word break corrected at 13.1, anow/ανου tagging at 9.16, 30 by David Parker.
Ghost pages removed and placement of ab/gap across page boundaries fixed by Catherine Smith.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 (apart from handling of ghost pages) by Hugh Houghton.
Page numbers fixed by Hugh Houghton and compared with transcription of pages in printed volume.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0 with manual adjustments.

ab.note: There is no extant page number. The page should be numbered 690 but the scribe has added 11 to the next extant page number so this page number has been reconstructed on that basis.
There is no extant page number. The page should be numbered 698 but the scribe has added 11 to the next extant page number so this page number has been reconstructed on that basis.
